Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client who uploads accounting documents.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the client was created.

AccountingDocument.title: AccountingDocument
AccountingDocument.description: Represents an accounting document uploaded by a client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the accounting document.

documentType.description: Type of accounting document (e.g., invoice, receipt, bank statement).

uploadDate.description: Date when the document was uploaded.

filePath.description: Path to the uploaded document file.

status.description: Current status of the document processing (e.g., uploaded, processing, validated, forwarded).

ExtractedData.title: ExtractedData
ExtractedData.description: Represents the extracted data from an accounting document.

id.description: Unique identifier for the extracted data.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Represents a log entry for an action performed in the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry.

userId.description: Identifier of the user who performed the action (can be null for system actions).

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the action was performed.

action.description: Description of the action performed (e.g., document uploaded, data extracted, data validated).

details.description: Additional details about the action performed (e.g., document ID, data changes).

definition.description: Stores client information.  The 'clientId' is used for path-based ownership in subcollections.
Stores accounting documents uploaded by a client. Path-based ownership is used for security.

params.name: accountingDocumentId
params.description: Unique identifier for the accounting document.

definition.description: Stores extracted data from an accounting document. Path-based ownership is used for security.

params.name: accountingDocumentId
params.description: Unique identifier for the accounting document.

params.name: extractedDataId
params.description: Unique identifier for the extracted data.

definition.description: Stores audit log entries for actions performed in the application.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability, aligning with the specified principles. Each client has a dedicated space for their documents, enabling straightforward security rules based on path-based ownership. 

Clients are stored in a top-level `/clients` collection. Each client owns their accounting documents, stored in a subcollection `/clients/{clientId}/accountingDocuments`. Extracted data for each accounting document resides in the `/clients/{clientId}/accountingDocuments/{accountingDocumentId}/extractedData` subcollection. This nested structure establishes a clear ownership hierarchy, simplifying security rules and eliminating the need for complex `get()` calls.

Audit logs are stored in a top-level `/auditLogs` collection. Because audit logs can be created for both clients and system-level actions, the userId field is included in each document. Although there is only one audit log collection, it is not considered Structural Segregation because the `userId` field allows for rules to be filtered based on the requesting user.

This structure facilitates straightforward security rules because:

1.  **Authorization Independence:** Path-based ownership (`/clients/{clientId}/...`) removes the need to fetch parent document data to authorize access.
2.  **QAPs:** Listing documents is secure as rules can easily filter by the `clientId` parameter, preventing unauthorized access.
3.  **Structural Segregation:**  Each collection/subcollection contains documents with homogeneous security needs, further simplifying rules.

No custom claims are used. The structure solely relies on `request.auth.uid` for authorization in conjunction with the path-based ownership model.
